$comment: Elevation boundary matrix — classifies every user-facing action by admin requirement. All WPF desktop actions operate on per-user state and run in the current standard-user session.

executionLevel.notes: The legacy PowerShell script and PS2EXE exe self-elevate immediately. The WPF shell launches as invoker and runs every backend action in the current standard-user token without a UAC relaunch.

actions.description: Compares pinned dependency versions against upstream; reads only
actions.notes: Runs in the same standard-user backend path as every other WPF action

actions.description: Downloads and applies SpotX + Spicetify customizations to Spotify
actions.notes: Primary install workflow. Spotify, Spicetify, and LibreSpot state used by the desktop workflow is per-user

actions.description: Refreshes SpotX patches and restores saved Spicetify state
Reinstalls Marketplace custom app, applies, and opens marketplace
Opens spotify:app:marketplace when Marketplace is already installed
actions.notes: Open-only action; repair remains the mutating path

actions.description: Runs spicetify restore to disable customizations — reversible via Reapply

actions.notes: Creates a Spicetify config backup without modifying Spotify
Restores Spicetify backup and reapplies — modifies Spotify files

actions.description: Removes Spicetify customizations while keeping SpotX in place

actions.notes: Registers a per-user task using InteractiveToken and LeastPrivilege

actions.description: Headless watcher that reapplies patches after Spotify updates
actions.notes: Runs as a per-user scheduled task at logon with LeastPrivilege

actions.description: Restores Spotify, removes Spicetify CLI, config, and PATH entry
8-phase comprehensive uninstaller: process kill, AppX, filesystem, registry, tasks, firewall, verification
Unregisters the watcher scheduled task and removes LibreSpot config, backups, logs, and crash reports. Does not affect Spotify or Spicetify.
actions.notes: Marked IsDestructive in AppCatalog. Does not require admin because it only removes per-user LibreSpot data.

actions.description: Removes LibreSpot's verified download cache and writes a local operation receipt.
actions.notes: Does not require admin because it only removes per-user LibreSpot cache data.

actions.description: Generates a structured preflight plan for the current config without mutating disk, PATH, tasks, or registry. Emits plan entries via the event protocol.
actions.notes: Read-only dry-run surface. Does not require admin. Fleet CLI exposes this as --dry-run.

nonActionWorkflows.notes: Config UI is available without elevation in the WPF shell
Windows app notifications are not supported for elevated processes
URI activation lands in the standard-user WPF shell and does not request elevation

designPrinciples: The WPF shell launches non-elevated (asInvoker) and runs all backend actions in the current standard-user token
Per-user permission failures are reported by the backend; the desktop shell never relaunches through UAC
Destructive actions (FullReset, UninstallSpicetify, RemoveSelfData) remain gated behind explicit confirmation
Toast and notification support remains available because the WPF process is not elevated
The legacy PowerShell script self-elevates immediately because its architecture does not separate read-only and mutating paths
